content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from agent research) can be repurposed to actively prune and prioritize long-term memory storage for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, rewarding retention of surprising, under-learned state observations over redundant, well-understood data. This creates a self-sustaining memory cycle where the agent’s natural curiosity continuously shapes its own memory footprint to improve active learning efficiency.
